chunks.page_summary: This page introduces the Ethernet connection capabilities of Rheonics SME sensor electronics, specifically detailing the SME-DRM model's use of standard Ethernet cables and RJ45 connectors.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Introducing Ethernet connections on SME
Modified on: Fri, 30 Jan, 2026 at 3:21 PM

All Rheonics sensor electronics come with an ethernet port. However whether it is active depends on the communication options you selected during the order.

SME-DRM Ethernet Cable Connection
SME-DRM is the DIN rail mount version of the sensor electronics which can use any standard Cat5, Cat6, Cat6e ethernet cables. The RJ45 connector is standard.
key_facts: All Rheonics sensor electronics are equipped with an Ethernet port.
The SME-DRM is the DIN rail mount version of the Rheonics sensor electronics.
The SME-DRM is compatible with any standard Cat5, Cat6, or Cat6e Ethernet cables.
The RJ45 connector is the standard Ethernet connector for the SME-DRM.
The device depicted is Swiss-made and bears a CE compliance mark.
Rheonics contact phone number: +1 713 234 0863.
This document was last modified on Friday, January 30, 2026, at 3:21 PM.
warnings_notes: The activity of the Ethernet port on Rheonics sensor electronics depends on the communication options selected during the order.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions and recommendations for connecting Ethernet cables to Rheonics SME-TR and SME-TRD devices, illustrating the process with a focus on the SME-DRM electronics.

Sensor connection

SME-TR Ethernet Cable Connection
Use a cable gland to run the ethernet cable through and then connect the ethernet cable to the electronics as shown in the pictures above for the SME-DRM.

SME-TRD Ethernet Cable Connection
SME-TRD uses a transmitter style housing with window cover. For SME-TR, there is no issue using a standard ethernet cable. However in SME-TRD with a display and glass window cover, a standard ethernet cable is too stiff to bend underneath the display.
For SME-TRD with display, we recommend that you use flat ethernet cable as shown in the photos below.
key_facts: Ethernet cable connection is demonstrated using the SME-DRM electronics.
SME-TR devices can use a standard Ethernet cable without issue.
SME-TRD devices feature a transmitter-style housing with a window cover and a display.
For SME-TRD devices with a display and glass window cover, a standard Ethernet cable is too stiff to bend underneath the display.
A flat Ethernet cable is recommended for SME-TRD devices with a display.
warnings_notes: For SME-TRD with a display, a standard ethernet cable is too stiff to bend underneath the display, so a flat ethernet cable is recommended.

key_facts: The Rheonics SME-EH device provides three channels for 4-20 mA analog output.
The device supports eight distinct sensor connections, numbered 0 through 8.
Available communication interfaces include Ethernet, USB, and Modbus (RS485).
The device is powered by a 24 V dc supply.
Additional ports are available for a display, an SD card, and a reset function.
Several indicator LEDs labeled D, C, S, P, B, A are present, with some currently illuminated green and one orange.
The Ethernet connection utilizes a UTP CAT.6 flat cable that conforms to the EIA/TIA 568B.2 standard.
Partial certification markings for IECE and TUV are visible on the device.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes the method for connecting an Ethernet cable to the SME-TR/TRD transmitter using a cable gland to ensure appropriate IP protection.
chunks.extracted_text:
Ethernet cable is then run through the side ports using a cable gland.

Ensuring IPXX rating on the ethernet cable connection to the SME-TR/TRD transmitter

To ensure IP protection, the ethernet cable should be run through a cable gland.
You have multiple options to run the ethernet cable through cable gland.
key_facts: The Ethernet cable is run through the side ports of the device using a cable gland.
Running the Ethernet cable through a cable gland is essential for ensuring IP protection (IPXX rating) for the connection to the SME-TR/TRD transmitter.
Multiple options exist for routing the Ethernet cable through a cable gland.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides two options for cable glands, one using a split sealing insert for flat ribbon cables and another using an Ethernet RJ45 cable gland adapter, with references to specific products and vendors.
product_names: Adafruit Cable Gland - Waterproof RJ-45 / Ethernet connector - RJ-45
chunks.extracted_text:
Option 1: Use a cable gland with a split sealing insert for flat ribbon cables
https://www.jacob-gmbh.de/en/products/cable-entry-systems/cable-glands/cable-glands-with-metric-thread/cable-glands-made-of-brass/perfect-cable-gland-506xx-mfk1 (https://www.jacob-gmbh.de/en/products/cable-entry-systems/cable-glands/cable-glands-with-metric-thread/cable-glands-made-of-brass/perfect-cable-gland-506xx-mfk1)

Option 2: Use a ethernet RJ45 cable gland adapter.
An example of ethernet RJ45 cable gland connector/adapter is:
Adafruit
Cable Gland - Waterproof RJ-45 / Ethernet connector - RJ-45
PRODUCT ID: 827
https://www.adafruit.com/product/827 (https://www.adafruit.com/product/827)
key_facts: Option 1 describes using a cable gland with a split sealing insert for flat ribbon cables.
Option 2 describes using an Ethernet RJ45 cable gland adapter.
A specific Jacob GmbH product, 'perfect-cable-gland-506xx-mfk1', is suggested for Option 1.
An Adafruit product, 'Cable Gland - Waterproof RJ-45 / Ethernet connector - RJ-45' with Product ID 827, is suggested for Option 2.

chunks.page_summary: This page displays two photographic views of a ruggedized industrial Ethernet RJ45 connector, showing both its RJ45 port and cable entry side.
key_facts: The page features a black, multi-part connector assembly.
The connector is designed for Ethernet connectivity, with an 8-pin RJ45 port visible on one end.
The body of the connector has prominent threading, suggesting it is intended for panel mounting or secure threaded coupling.
The design implies a ruggedized or environmentally sealed application, likely for industrial or outdoor use.
The other end of the connector assembly is configured for cable entry, appearing to include a cable gland or strain relief mechanism.

chunks.page_summary: This page details 'Option 3' for connecting internal and external ethernet cables using an RJ45 coupler and a cable gland, illustrating the components involved.
chunks.extracted_text:
Video in attachment show the connection of the internal and external ethernet cables. Internal cable is a flat ribbon cable as mentioned above which is easy to bend underneath the display. External cable is a standard ethernet roung cable that seals using the cable gland.

There are multiple other vendors for this type of Ethernet RJ45 cable gland connectors.

Option 3: Using a coupler and standard ethernet cable through a typical cable gland

This method uses a coupler to connect the flat ethernet cable to the external ethernet cable.

Example: https://www.belden.com/products/connectors/copper-rj45-connectors/copper-rj45-couplers
(https://www.belden.com/products/connectors/copper-rj45-connectors/copper-rj45-couplers)
Run the external ethernet cable through cable gland.
key_facts: A video in attachment demonstrates the connection of internal and external ethernet cables.
The internal cable is a flat ribbon cable, designed for easy bending underneath the display.
The external cable is a standard round ethernet cable that is sealed using a cable gland.
Multiple vendors offer Ethernet RJ45 cable gland connectors.
Option 3 involves using a coupler and a standard ethernet cable to pass through a typical cable gland.
This method connects the flat internal ethernet cable to the external ethernet cable via a coupler.
Belden copper RJ45 couplers are provided as an example product.
diagram_labels: Belden RJ45 keystone/coupler modules (silver, black, black/orange)

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions for connecting an external ethernet cable to a Rheonics SMET device using an ethernet coupler and references additional communication options.
chunks.extracted_text:
Connect the external ethernet cable to the internal flat ethernet cable using the ethernet coupler. Flat ethernet cable is connected to the SMET.

References

Explore rheonics sensors' electronics and communication options
https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/ (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/)

Attachments
key_facts: An external ethernet cable should be connected to the internal flat ethernet cable using an ethernet coupler.
The flat ethernet cable is connected to the SMET.
Further information on Rheonics sensors' electronics and communication options can be found at https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/.
